items.title: Scrap RM3.5mil MP allocation to boost savings, says Pas leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Pas leader has proposed that the government abolish the special allocation of RM3.5 million distributed to each government member of parliament as a cost-saving measure amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The party's Information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said if such a step were implemented, it would result in far greater savings compared with the proposal to reduce MPs' petrol allowances of RM1,500. "The savings would be greater if the RM3.5 million special allocation is abolished.

items.title: Brain drain worsens as trainee doctors turn to Singapore
items.summary: A research note by MBSB Research revealed that of the 5,000 housemanship placements offered by the Health Ministry in January 2026 to absorb the surplus of graduates, only 529 individuals, or roughly 10.5 per cent, reported for duty. The situation reflected an escalating talent drain, particularly as neighbouring Singapore adjusted its strategy to attract medical specialists from Malaysia. Among the institutions targeted were Universiti Malaya, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia and Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM).

items.title: More US naval forces arrive in Middle East
items.summary: WASHINGTON: More US sailors and marines have arrived in West Asia aboard the USS Tripoli, an amphibious assault ship, the US military said on Saturday, reported German Press Agency (dpa). The forces are joining thousands of US marines already deployed to Middle East as part of a massive military build-up ahead of the Iran war. The US and Israel launched joint attacks on Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Police detain two PKR men over alleged extortion in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two men who allegedly extorted a massage parlour with "protection money" in Wangsa Maju were detained by the police. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus confirmed the arrests, Malaysiakini reported. On Friday, Datuk Fadil said police were investigating the case after receiving three reports regarding the matter.

items.title: Mount Semeru erupts nine times today, triggers pyroclastic flows
items.summary: JAKARTA: Mount Semeru in East Java erupted nine times today, with the latest eruption sending an ash column up to 800 metres above its summit and triggering pyroclastic flows that travelled 3.5 kilometres down its southeastern slopes. Mukdas said the strongest eruption occurred at 1.01am, producing an ash column reaching approximately 1,000 metres above the summit, with moderate intensity drifting southwest. Authorities said Mount Semeru remains at Level III (Alert) status.

items.title: Sultan crosses paths with accident victim he once helped
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, known for his caring nature in assisting accident victims while travelling, yesterday crossed paths with a person he had helped following a crash on the East Coast Highway earlier this year. The unexpected reunion took place during the Bera parliamentary constituency and Koperasi Serbausaha Makmur Bhd Aidilfitri open house celebration in Bera, where the motorcyclist, identified as Faris Farhan, met Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah. Recounting the incident, His Royal Highness, who later addressed the crowd, said he had come across an accident victim whom he had assisted previously.

items.title: Petronas denies Philippines fuel supply deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has denied any involvement in a proposed fuel supply arrangement in the Philippines. "Petronas' immediate focus is to ensure a reliable and continuous fuel supply for Malaysia. Philippine media reported on Friday that Davao del Norte province in Mindanao plans to import 44 million litres of crude oil and gasoline from Malaysia to curb rising oil prices.

items.title: Ex-soldier describes close encounter with death while hunting communists
items.summary: Yusof began his service as a lance corporal with the 11th Battalion Police Field Force in 1969. Yusof said he was travelling in one of the military lorries transporting personnel through the rugged terrain. he said.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry seizes RM32m in petrol and diesel, arrest 667 under Ops Tiris 3.0
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, March 29 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) seized petrol and diesel worth more than RM32 million and arrested 667 people under the Ops Tiris 3.0 conducted nationwide from January 1, 2024 to March 27 this year. KPDN deputy director-general of enforcement (operations) Shamsul Nizam Khalil said that during the period, 38,615 inspections were carried out and 1,371 cases were recorded. “KPDN will continue to intensify integrated operations to curb any leakage, smuggling and misappropriation of petrol and diesel.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm and strong‑wind alert for multiple states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several areas in the Peninsula, Sarawak and Sabah until 5pm today. MetMalaysia said the states expected to be affected in the Peninsula are Penang (Southwest, Northeast and Seberang Perai Utara); Perak (Batang Padang and Muallim); Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub and Rompin); and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru). In Sabah, the areas likely to be hit are the interior regions (Sipitang, Tenom, Beaufort and Nabawan), the West Coast (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud) and Tawau (Tawau and Lahad Datu).

items.title: Malaysia to bar foreign debit and credit cards from RON95 purchases to curb subsidy misuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The use of foreign credit and debit cards to purchase RON95 petrol nationwide will reportedly be restricted in stages starting Wednesday. “Previously, there were many cases of vehicles with foreign registration plates refuelling using foreign debit or credit cards at self-service pumps, making it difficult for operators and authorities to detect such purchases,” he was quoted as saying. He reportedly said foreign card users will instead be required to pay at the counter, allowing better monitoring and enforcement.

items.title: Police open murder probe after discovery of charred body, burnt motorcycle in Kulim
items.summary: KULIM, March 29 — Police have opened a murder investigation following the discovery of a charred body alongside a burnt motorcycle at Ladang Anak Kulim yesterday. Kulim police chief Zulkifli Azizan said the Terap Police Station received a call at 2.25 pm from a member of the public who stumbled upon the body while in the area. “The individual had gone to the location to catch birds and had stumbled upon the burnt body and motorcycle.

items.title: Terengganu schools urged to provide sufficient drinking water amid hot weather
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: The Terengganu State Education Department (JPNT) encouraged all students to bring sufficient drinking water amid the ongoing hot weather. "We have also held discussions with dining hall operators at boarding schools to ensure enough drinking water is available. Kamsah said schools have also been instructed to provide water stations at designated locations to make it easier for students to access drinking water nearby.

items.title: Two arrested over murder after burnt body found at Kulim plantation
items.summary: KULIM: Police have arrested two men to assist in a murder investigation following the discovery of a burnt body at a plantation here yesterday. Kulim police chief Superintendent Zulkifli Azizan said the witness spotted the body from a hilly area. Police arrested two men, aged 22 and 68, at 1am today.

items.title: Family of eight escapes as fire destroys home in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA: A family of eight narrowly escaped injury after a fire destroyed most of their home in Kampung Duyong last night. "We rushed home, but almost the entire house had already been destroyed," she said. Noridayu said the family is now staying temporarily at her brother's home in Durian Tunggal.

items.title: Two men in Wangsa Maju ‘protection money’ video arrested, police probe ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Two men seen in a viral video allegedly demanding protection money from a business operator have been arrested by police. According to a report by Malaysiakini , Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus confirmed the arrests last night, saying investigations are ongoing. Federal Territories PKR leadership council chairperson Azman Abidin was quoted as saying the resignations were intended to ensure transparency throughout the process.

items.title: Japan seeks to expand investment in Malaysia's digital, high-tech sectors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Japan is aiming to expand its investment in Malaysia's digital industry, encompassing niche areas such as high-tech chip products and data centres. Kuremura said that investment in the retail space from Japan to Malaysia is also anticipated to increase in 2026. Kuremura said that Japan is also providing a matching platform to promote investment collaboration for start-ups in Japan and Asean.

items.title: West Asia conflict may spur domestic tourism growth in Sarawak, says state minister
items.summary: KUCHING, March 29 — The ongoing conflicts in West Asia are not expected to significantly impact Malaysia’s tourism sector and could even provide a boost, particularly through increased domestic travel, said Sarawak Minister of Tourism, Creative Industries and Performing Arts Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. He said that global uncertainties often encourage people to travel within the country rather than abroad, indirectly supporting the local tourism industry. “Tourism can be divided into international and domestic segments.

items.title: Malaysia, China seen as Asia's more resilient economies amid energy market volatility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and China are emerging as relatively resilient economies in Asia amid intensifying global energy market volatility due to the ongoing conflict in Middle East. JP Morgan head of Asia and co-head of global emerging markets equity strategy, Rajiv Batra, told CNBC in an interview that, beyond China and Malaysia, most economies in the region appear more vulnerable to energy shocks. He said Malaysia's strength lies in its energy export profile and disciplined policy framework, which provide a buffer against external pressures.

items.title: Kedah to extend PdPR if hot weather continues
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The implementation of Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) in areas recording Level 2 daily temperatures in Kedah will be extended if the hot weather persists. Kedah Education director Abdul Rahim Mat said the department is closely monitoring daily temperature readings issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) before making any decisions. A total of 86,030 students were involved, comprising 65,634 students from 154 schools in Kota Star and 20,396 students from 52 schools in Pendang.

items.title: 5 things you didn't know about iPhone
items.summary: PARIS: iPhone maker Apple celebrates its 50th anniversary on April 1, having marked pop culture and the tech industry like few other firms since its beginnings in 1976. APPLE LOGO Designer Rob Janoff said Apple co-founder Steve Jobs gave him one terse instruction when he commissioned a new logo in January 1977: "don't make it cute". APPLE'S THIRD MAN Jobs and Steve Wozniak have gone down in history as Apple co-founders.

items.title: No need for PdPR in Terengganu for now
items.summary: Terengganu Education director Kamsah @ Kamal Mohamed said the state is not listed among those at very high risk of experiencing extreme heat. "The authority to implement PdPR or decide on school uniform policies lies with the schools and the department. He said if weather conditions become excessively hot, the department will authorise temporary school closures, during which PdPR will be implemented.

items.title: Indian tile makers feel heat of Mideast war energy crunch
items.summary: MORBI: Blazing-hot kilns in India's US$6.5 billion ceramics manufacturing hub employing hundreds of thousands of people have gone cold, shut down in an energy crunch caused by the Middle East war. At a cavernous facility in Morbi, in India's western Gujarat state, a 200-metre-long propane-powered kiln that normally fires clay nonstop is silent. India, the world's fourth-largest economy, depends on imports for 60 per cent of its liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) demand.

items.title: Traders all smiles as Kuantan heatwave sends watermelon sales soaring
items.summary: KUANTAN: The heatwave that has affected the district over the past few weeks has turned out to be a blessing for watermelon traders operating along Jalan Kuantan–Pekan near Kampung Ubai here. Farmer Husaini Abdullah, 49, said he sells between 300 and 400kg of watermelon daily, with a steady stream of customers frequenting his stall in recent days. The 49-year-old, who operates a 1.1ha farm in Kampung Ubai, said the boci (seedless) variety of watermelon has been a favourite among customers.

items.title: Kelantan records 1,089 open burning cases since Jan 1, says Fire Dept
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 29 — The Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has recorded 1,089 open burning cases from the overall total of 1,418 fire emergency calls received since January 1. Its director, Farhan Sufyan Borhan, said that of the 1,089 open burning cases, 880 were bush fires, forest fires (114), garbage fires (70) and plantation fires (25), totalling 1,618 hectares statewide. To tackle these open burning cases, he said a total of 28 fire engines, seven 10,000-litre and 20,000-litre water tankers and a hydro-sub vehicle are deployed at 22 fire stations throughout the state.

items.title: How Malaysia can help build a smarter Global South economy
items.summary: And Malaysia should not be a spectator. It must help build it through concrete, strategic business partnerships that deepen industrial capacity across the developing world. Take palm oil.

items.title: Poultry firm fined RM30,000 over illegal effluent discharge in Kedah
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: A chicken slaughtering company in Bukit Pinang has been fined RM30,000 by the Sessions Court for failing to comply with industrial effluent discharge standards. The Kedah Department of Environment (DOE) said the company pleaded guilty to two charges under the Environmental Quality Act 1974. Its director, Sharifah Zakiah Syed Sahab, said the offences involved the discharge of effluent exceeding permitted limits.

items.title: 78 litterbugs ordered to perform community service across six states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 78 litterbugs were ordered to undergo Community Service Orders (CSO) yesterday for littering offences under the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act. The community service orders focused on comprehensive cleaning works, including pavements, public roads, drainage systems (scupper drains), public toilets and coastal areas. To date, the Solid Waste Management and Public Cleansing Corporation (SWCorp) has issued 1,342 Notices of Offence (NPK) across seven states under the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act, involving 1,058 Malaysians and 284 foreigners.

items.title: AmInvestment keeps 'Buy' on Kim Loong despite Q4 earnings dip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kim Loong Resources Bhd saw higher wage costs in the fourth quarter (Q4), mainly due to the increase in minimum wage and Employees Provident Fund contributions for foreign workers. AmInvestment Bank Bhd said the group's earnings fell 17.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter in Q4, weighed down by lower palm product prices as well as higher wages and transportation costs. Transportation costs rose following restrictions on truck loading limits.

items.title: Family of 11 escapes blaze that engulfs Ulu Yam home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eleven people managed to escape before a fire engulfed a single-storey family home in Kampung Pasir, Ulu Yam Baru, late last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (operations) Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received a distress call at 11.21pm and dispatched personnel from the Kuala Kubu Baru station, with assistance from Bukit Sentosa and Rawang. He said 14 personnel were involved in the operation, supported by two fire rescue tenders and a water tanker.

items.title: Digital Ministry engages industry to improve Employment Pass processing for tech talent
items.summary: The ministry stressed that it takes a serious view of the concerns raised by industry stakeholders, with various engagement sessions being actively implemented in collaboration with relevant agencies, including the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC). The ministry said the GBS sector is one of the key drivers of the nation’s digital economy, contributing significantly to investment inflows, high-value job creation and the strengthening of Malaysia’s position as a competitive regional digital hub. “In addition, the MDEC is also engaging closely with key industry associations, such as GBS Malaysia (Digital Global Business Services Council Malaysia, a chapter of PIKOM) and Contact Centre Association of Malaysia (CCAM), to apprise stakeholders of the latest development,” it added.

items.title: Open burning drives 1,418 fire incidents in Kelantan
items.summary: Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department director Farhan Sufyan Borhan said 1,089 cases were classified as open burning, affecting an estimated 1,620ha statewide. "Of the total, 880 cases involved bush fires, 114 forest fires, 70 rubbish fires and 25 plantation fires," he said. Farhan said Kota Baru recorded the highest number of open burning cases at 400, followed by Bachok (278) and Pasir Mas (173).

items.title: Overloaded passenger boat detained off Pulau Redang
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained a passenger boat found to be carrying more than the permitted number of passengers during a patrol operation in the waters off Pulau Redang yesterday. Terengganu MMEA director Maritime Captain Hamiludin Che Awang said the boat was detained at about 3 pm, some 26 nautical miles from the mouth of Kuala Terengganu, after it was spotted carrying a large number of passengers. "An inspection found that the boat was carrying 25 passengers, compared with its licensed capacity of only 12 as stipulated in its valid licence," he said.

items.title: Police arrest five men after viral car‑smashing video in Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 29 — Police have detained five men over a viral video showing a group smashing a vehicle at a traffic light junction near Jesselton Quay here. Acting Kota Kinabalu district police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said a 30-year-old teacher lodged a police report at about 2.35 pm yesterday claiming to be the victim in the incident. “A verbal provocation took place before the situation escalated, causing the complainant to reverse his vehicle and inadvertently collide with another vehicle.

items.title: Genting Highlands restaurant charges RM902 for fish dish, draws KPDN scrutiny
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — A Genting Highlands restaurant has reportedly come under scrutiny after a group of diners were charged RM902 for a wild river fish dish. The restaurant reportedly said the fish was a rare wild river species and that the price was calculated based on weight, with no hidden or additional charges imposed. “When the customer ordered food that day, the staff had explained the variety, weight and price per 100 grams of fish, and the final charge was also calculated according to the actual weight, rather than arbitrarily quoting the price,” the restaurant operator was quoted as saying.

items.title: Malaysia seeks Egypt's help to deliver 374 tonnes of aid to Gaza
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has sought Egypt's help to deliver 374 tonnes of humanitarian aid to Gaza via the Rafah border crossing starting this April. "I asked for his cooperation to deliver 374 tonnes of Malaysian humanitarian aid to Gaza via the Rafah border crossing starting this April." Anwar said Malaysia would not bow to pressure, especially from Israel, which he said continues to hinder humanitarian efforts.

items.title: 108,000 litres dropped as Rompin plantation fire enters seventh day
items.summary: The Pahang Fire and Rescue Department said 135 water-bombing runs were carried out at several hotspots from yesterday to noon today. He said about 300 hectares of a 1,214-hectare plantation had been affected, with 59 hectares successfully extinguished so far, including 19 hectares yesterday. Of that, 520 hectares have been extinguished, including around 200 hectares yesterday, leaving 196 hectares still burning.

items.title: Hot weather not affecting water release at four Perak dams, says state exco
items.summary: IPOH, March 29 — Water release at the four main dams in Perak is at a controlled level and is still able to meet domestic needs, despite several districts in the state recording Level One hot weather over the past few days. Perak Infrastructure, Energy, Water and Public Transport Committee chairman Mohammad Nizar Jamaluddin said that currently, water release at Temengor Dam, Bersia Dam, Chenderoh Dam and Kenering Dam, which are fully supervised by Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), is above the minimum level of around 120 cubic metres per second. “Indirectly, the surplus can be utilised for domestic use including ensuring that the supply of treated water remains adequate throughout Perak,” he said when contacted by Bernama today.

items.title: Traffic slows on major highways as return travel to Kuala Lumpur continues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic was slow and congested on several major highways nationwide as of midday today, as return travel to the capital continued. Updates from the East Coast Expressway Phase 1 showed congestion on westbound routes between Bentong and Lentang, as well as around Bukit Tinggi, with traffic building up over stretches of between 800m and 1.4km. Meanwhile, PLUS Malaysia Berhad reported slow traffic along several stretches of the North-South Expressway and connected routes in both directions.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming: 78 people complete community service for littering across six states
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 29 — A total of 78 individuals underwent Community Service Orders (CSOs) for minor littering offences in six states yesterday, said Minister of Housing and Local Government, Nga Kor Ming. “These CSOs focus on comprehensive cleaning efforts, covering sidewalks, public roads, drainage systems, public toilets and beaches,” he said in a statement today. Nga added that the orders are part of an integrated enforcement strategy to curb littering in public spaces, stressing that if minor littering is left unchecked, it can clog drains, inconvenience local communities and even trigger flash floods in urban areas.

items.title: Germany's WWII munitions a toxic legacy on Baltic Sea floor
items.summary: KIEL, (Germany): Below the waves off Germany's northern tourist beaches, a toxic time bomb lurks on the Baltic Sea floor – enormous quantities of World War II munitions that are slowly rusting away. To better map the dangers, a research vessel set sail this month from the port city of Kiel, whose bay is among the most polluted with unexploded ordnance. Along the German coast, about 1.6 million tonnes of munitions litter the seafloor, especially near the ports of Kiel and Luebeck, making it one of the world's most contaminated areas.

items.title: Body of missing fisherman discovered along Tanjung Rhu shoreline
items.summary: MERSING, March 29 — A fisherman reported missing yesterday was found drowned along the shoreline near Tanjung Rhu early this morning. Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department senior fire officer II Ikmal Hafiz Shamsudin said the 41-year-old man was discovered by a member of the public at 1.45 am. Eight personnel from the Mersing Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene with a Light Fire Rescue Tender and a utility vehicle.

items.title: Malaysia steps up food security measures as West Asia conflict disrupts supply chains, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — The government will continue to strengthen the country’s food system to ensure sufficient supply and access to the people during the conflict in West Asia. Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said it is a responsibility that we are currently carrying out. He said that in today’s world, the geopolitical conflict in West Asia is not just an external issue, as it also affects the global food supply chain.

items.title: MOF: Govt maintains fuel subsidies to cushion impact of rising global oil prices
items.summary: In a Facebook post today, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said the government is continuing its efforts through the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) and the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS), which provide targeted fuel subsidies for the land and water public transport sectors, as well as for the transportation of selected goods. “These measures help keep transportation costs under control and ensure that commodity prices remain stable,” the ministry said. Meanwhile, under SKDS, the subsidised diesel price for the land transport sector remains at RM1.88 per litre, while diesel for goods transportation continues to be capped at RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Concerns grow over aggressive 'freelance photographers' at KLCC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Concerns are mounting over the behaviour of unlicensed photographers operating around the Petronas Twin Towers, with visitors reporting aggressive tactics and growing unease at one of the city's busiest tourist spots. Groups of men offering paid photography services — often approaching both local and foreign visitors — have been active in the area, particularly in the evenings, prompting questions over enforcement and public safety. Some visitors said the photographers compete for customers, at times creating a tense atmosphere.

items.title: Anwar: Egypt guarantees smooth passage for Malaysian aid into Gaza
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Egypt has given a clear guarantee and commitment to facilitate the distribution of 374 tonnes of humanitarian aid from Malaysia to the Palestinian population in Gaza through the Rafah border crossing starting this April, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “Alhamdulillah, President El-Sisi has given a clear guarantee and commitment to ensure that this access is facilitated, thus enabling immediate aid to be distributed to more than 100,000 Gazans who are in dire need,” he said in a Facebook post. “Please pray that all matters are facilitated and that this aid reaches our brothers in Gaza safely, God willing,” he said.

items.title: Universiti Malaya secures world-first dental accreditation from Royal College of Surgeons England
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Universiti Malaya (UM) has become the first institution globally to be accredited as a Dental Education Centre by the Faculty of Dental Surgery of the Royal College of Surgeons of England. In a statement today, UM said the award was presented by Faculty of Dental Surgery dean Dr Charlotte Eckhardt to Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Student Affairs) Professor Dr Zamri Radzi, who represented the vice-chancellor, together with Faculty of Dentistry dean Professor Dr Firdaus Hariri. UM said the recognition affirms that its dental speciality doctoral programmes meet rigorous academic and clinical standards set by the Faculty of Dental Surgery.

items.title: ‘No such arrangement’: Petronas denies report of alleged fuel supply deal in Philippines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has denied any involvement in a reported fuel supply deal linked to a local government unit in the Philippines. It added that its immediate priority remains ensuring a reliable and continuous fuel supply for Malaysia, supported by established supply networks and close coordination with the government, it said in a statement today. — Bernama

items.title: Kuantan pensioner loses RM337,000 in Facebook investment scam
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the woman contacted the suspect via WhatsApp and was instructed to carry out multiple financial transactions. He said she made 38 transfers into 16 different bank accounts using her savings and pawned jewellery between January 30 and March 9. “All investment-related matters, including profits, were handled solely via WhatsApp without any legitimate platform,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Malaysia taps AI, flood forensics to boost water security under AIR2040
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 29 — The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia (Nahrim), is intensifying efforts to strengthen national water security by leveraging science, technology and innovation in line with the 2040 Water Sector Transformation Agenda (AIR2040) under the 13th Malaysia Plan. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, who is also the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said a research-driven and evidence-based approach is central to ensuring sustainable and resilient water resource management, particularly in addressing climate change and rising water demand. Flood management is also being enhanced through surplus water storage technologies such as riverbed reservoirs, nature-based solutions and multi-functional reservoirs.

items.title: Rompin’s 300-hectare fire still burning after 85 water-bombing runs, say firefighters
items.summary: Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations and rescue, Mohd Salahuddin Isa, said 85 water-bombing runs had been carried out, using 68,000 litres of water to contain the blaze. Meanwhile, according to the Air Pollutant Index Management System, air quality in Rompin was unhealthy, with an Air Pollutant Index (API) reading of 152 as of 10am. Pahang Department of Environment director Zakaria Ahmad said checks would be carried out to determine whether the fire or other factors caused the unhealthy API reading.

items.title: No tsunami risk to Malaysia after strong quake in Indonesia’s Banda Sea, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — There is no tsunami threat to Malaysia following a strong earthquake that struck Banda Sea, Indonesia, early this morning. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the strong 6.0 magnitude earthquake occurred at 2.38am. He said the epicentre of the earthquake was located at coordinates 7.9 degrees south and 127.8 degrees east at a depth of 173 kilometres (km).

items.title: Deputy minister eyes direct Miri-China charter flights in tourism push
items.summary: MIRI, March 29 — The Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry recently held an engagement session with Chinese tour operators involved in developing charter flight packages. Deputy Minister Chiew Choon Man, who delivered a presentation during a segment of the programme, said he encouraged the operators to explore new direct flight routes between Miri and China. My goal is to put Miri firmly on the global tourism map, especially as one of Sarawak’s largest cities.

items.title: Nearly 1,500 found flouting traffic rules in Penang during 13-day Aidilfitri ops
items.summary: Its director, Zulkifly Ismail, said 19,900 vehicles were inspected during the period, with 1,492 found violating traffic rules. A total of 13 vehicles were also seized for various offences. “Private vehicles recorded the highest number of cases at 1,860, followed by motorcycles (500), goods vehicles exceeding 7,500 kg (253), goods vehicles below 7,500 kg (83), and public service vehicles, including buses (19),” he said.

items.title: Parking dispute turns violent in Kota Kinabalu, teacher injured in alleged mob attack
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 29 — A minor parking mishap escalated into a violent mob attack, leaving a teacher injured and traumatised in what he described as a “lawless” ordeal. The incident occurred yesterday afternoon near a shipping company warehouse close to the Kota Kinabalu port, where the victim had parked amid heavy congestion during the state government Aidilfitri open house at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC). In a panic, he said attempted to leave but accidentally struck another vehicle while reversing.

items.title: Motorcyclist, 17, dies in Besut crash after attempted overtake at junction
items.summary: JERTIH, March 29 — A boy, 17, was killed when the motorcycle he was riding hit another motorcycle in Kampung Telaga Nibong, Hulu Besut near here yesterday. Besut district police chief Supt Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said Muhammad Haziq Azman, a chicken sales assistant, died at the scene while the other motorcyclist suffered minor injuries during the 11.30am crash. “But at the same time, the victim, on a Honda Wave 125 came from behind and tried to overtake the Honda EX5,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Sarawak air quality worsens as 17 areas record moderate API levels
items.summary: “The increase in API levels is likely due to localised open burning and transboundary haze. If dry weather conditions persist and biomass burning activities increase, Sarawak risks experiencing haze,” the statement read. “In this regard, NREB has taken various proactive measures, including activating its haze operations room, conducting field patrols, temporarily suspending the issuance of controlled burning permits and issuing notices prohibiting open burning to all project developers.

items.title: Police probe alleged cable theft in Ipoh after video goes viral on TikTok
items.summary: IPOH, March 29 — Police are seeking public assistance over a suspected cable theft at a residential area in Jalan Pakatan Jaya, Taman Pakatan Jaya here, which has gone viral on social media. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said police came across the video on TikTok, uploaded by the account Utusan Melayu Plus (@utusanmelayuplusofficial) at about 11am yesterday. “Police have opened an investigation paper and the case is being investigated under Section 379 of the Penal Code,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Heatwave forces 206 schools in Kedah to switch to home learning
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 29 — Schools in Kota Setar and Pendang will shift to Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) for three days, from today until Tuesday, due to the ongoing heatwave. The Kedah State Education Department (JPN) said the decision follows reports from the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), which recorded Level 2 heatwave temperatures in both districts from March 26 to 28. The Kedah JPN will continue to monitor weather conditions and provide updates on school operations as needed.

items.title: Police nab Chinese national for allegedly filming man in Bukit Jalil toilet mall
items.summary: Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect, in his 20s, was nabbed at 1.28 pm following a report lodged by the victim. “Following the arrest, police seized the suspect’s mobile phone, and a preliminary check found several lewd videos in the gallery,” he said in a statement yesterday. Mohd Rosdi added that initial investigations revealed the suspect admitted to the act, while further checks showed he did not possess valid travel documents.
